title: Landing Stiffly
subtitle: Absorb the impact by bending your knees immediately upon landing.

title: Rounding Back
subtitle: Keep your spine neutral; avoid hunching over.

title: Incomplete Twist
subtitle: Commit to the full 180-degree rotation for maximum effectiveness.

title: Using Momentum
subtitle: Perform the rep slowly to ensure muscle engagement.

title: Pulling Neck
subtitle: Keep your neck neutral; don't yank it forward.

title: Limited ROM
subtitle: Go through the full range of motion for best results.

title: Arching Lower Back
subtitle: Stop before your back sags; this causes injury.

title: Using Hips Only
subtitle: Movement should come from extending the torso, not just shifting hips.

title: Looking Up
subtitle: Keep your gaze down to maintain a neutral neck.

title: Partial Reps
subtitle: Open your legs wide to get a full stretch.

title: Helping with Hands
subtitle: Keep hands on handles, don't push your legs with them.

title: Stepping on Rungs
subtitle: Aim for the center of the spaces.

title: Looking Down
subtitle: Try to look slightly ahead, not staring directly at feet.

title: Flat Feet
subtitle: Stay on your toes for agility.

title: Heels Lifting
subtitle: Keep heels glued to the floor.

title: Rounding Back
subtitle: Keep your chest proud and core tight.

title: Straining Neck
subtitle: Lift with your abs, not your neck muscles.

title: Too Fast
subtitle: Control the tempo to feel the contraction.

title: Shoulders on Floor
subtitle: Keep shoulder blades off the floor to maintain tension.

title: Narrow Stance
subtitle: Step wide enough to maintain balance (railroad tracks, not tightrope).

title: Front Knee Collapse
subtitle: Keep knee aligned with second toe.

title: Twisting Hips
subtitle: Try to keep hips facing forward.

title: Knee Pain
subtitle: Don't force the depth if it hurts your knees.

title: Loss of Balance
subtitle: Move slowly and focus on a fixed point.

title: Moving Leg
subtitle: Isolate the movement to the ankle joint.

title: Rushing
subtitle: Take your time to mobilize the joint.

title: Small Circles
subtitle: Aim for maximum range of motion.

title: Limited ROM
subtitle: Push and pull to the end of your range.

title: Flaring Elbows
subtitle: Puts stress on shoulders; keep them tucked.

title: Shoulders Shrugging
subtitle: Keep shoulders down and away from ears.

title: Going Too Deep
subtitle: Can injure shoulders; stop at parallel.

title: Hyperextending
subtitle: Stop when your body is straight.

title: Moving Too Fast
subtitle: Use control to avoid spinal injury.

title: Arm Pulling
subtitle: Power comes from hips, not arms.

title: Slow Elbows
subtitle: Turn elbows over quickly to catch.

title: Bar Swinging Out
subtitle: Modify path to keep bar vertical.

title: Rocking
subtitle: Don't rock back to gain momentum.

title: Relaxing on Box
subtitle: Keep muscles engaged; spinal compression risk.

title: Not Using Legs
subtitle: Use the leg drive for the 'push' part.

title: Muscle Clean
subtitle: Don't just use arms; use hip power.

title: Rounding Back
subtitle: Keep spine neutral to avoid injury.

title: Bar Drift
subtitle: Converting to squat; keep bar close.

title: Squatting
subtitle: This is a hinge, not a squat.

title: Pressing Movement
subtitle: This is an arc, not a press.

title: Heavy Weight
subtitle: Use very light or no weight.

title: Hips Tilting
subtitle: Imagine balancing a water connection on your back.

title: Upright Torso
subtitle: Can strain back leg hip flexor.

title: Knees Up
subtitle: This is High Knees, keep 'em down.
